rationale: In the theoretical framing, the paper cites Khan and Dhar (2006) for specific propositions: hedonic choices are guilt-laden and unrelated virtuous acts can serve as justifications that increase indulgent choice. That evidence is then used to motivate the core mechanism here, namely that a discount framed on the hedonic bundle component functions as a justification and raises bundle purchase. The citation is therefore doing conceptual work in hypothesis development rather than serving as a passing literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Khan and Dhar (2006) as prior empirical grounding for justification-based increases in hedonic consumption. It builds from that logic to propose and test a new pricing-context prediction: discount framing on the hedonic item should boost purchase of hedonic-utilitarian bundles.
